subject: Women pushing baby carriages in Városmajor park, Budapest XII district, 1938
context_for_lesson: A 1938 photograph of women pushing baby carriages in Városmajor park, Budapest's leafy 12th district. This is exactly the kind of peaceful pre-war Budapest scene Judy Abrams would have known as a small child — Judy was born in 1937, and her family lived on Stefánia út in Budapest's 14th district, just across the city from this park. The photograph anchors what 'normal life' looked like for a young Hungarian Jewish family one year after Judy's birth, before the dangers of 1944 forced her into hiding.
publisher_or_host: Wikimedia Commons (originally from FORTEPAN historical photo archive, donor: Lissák Tivadar)
author: FOTO:FORTEPAN / Lissák Tivadar

lesson_use_intent: Page 2 (Meet Judy) or Page 3 (Before We Read). Caption: 'Budapest, 1938 — peaceful park scenes like this one were the everyday world Judy Abrams was born into. She was one year old when this photo was taken.'
concerns: None. Pure pre-war daily-life imagery. No overlap with Holocaust imagery; serves the 'before' bookend perfectly.
